description: ether.fi is a liquid staking and restaking protocol where users deposit ETH into the LiquidityPool and receive eETH, a rebasing receipt token with a market capitalization of {{eethMarketCap}}. Deposited ETH is used to provision Ethereum validators through the StakingManager and EtherFiNodesManager, which run validators via EigenLayer EigenPods, and idle liquidity is restaked through EigenLayer strategies and Lido stETH by the EtherFiRestaker. The protocol is fully upgradeable: nearly every core contract is owned by an OpenZeppelin TimelockController with a 10-day delay, whose proposer, executor and canceller roles are held by a single 6-of-10 multisig. Validator accounting and rebases depend on the EtherFiOracle, and the protocol relies on EigenLayer for restaking, Lido for stETH withdrawals, and LayerZero for cross-chain messaging.

eth:0x9f26d4C958fD811A1F59B01B86Be7dFFc9.name: EtherFi Timelock (10-day delay)
eth:0x9f26d4C958fD811A1F59B01B86Be7dFFc9.description: OpenZeppelin TimelockController that directly owns 41 protocol contracts, controlling {{timelockDirectCapital}} in capital and {{timelockTokenValue}} in eETH token value. It can upgrade every core contract (LiquidityPool, StakingManager, EtherFiNodesManager, WithdrawRequestNFT, MembershipManager, EtherFiOracle and others), grant and revoke roles, change fee and rate parameters, and move funds via withdraw and sweep functions. All actions are subject to a 10-day (864000-second) minimum delay before execution.

eth:0xcdd57D11476c22d265722F68390b036f3D.name: EtherFi Governance Multisig (6-of-10)
eth:0xcdd57D11476c22d265722F68390b036f3D.description: Gnosis Safe holding the PROPOSER, EXECUTOR and CANCELLER roles on the EtherFi Timelock. It is the sole entity able to schedule, execute and cancel timelock operations, so it effectively controls every contract the timelock owns ({{governanceMultisigReachable}} in reachable capital), with all changes gated behind the 10-day timelock delay.

eth:0x2aCA71020De61bb532008049e1Bd41E451.name: ProxyAdmin Owner Multisig (4-of-7)
eth:0x2aCA71020De61bb532008049e1Bd41E451.description: Gnosis Safe that owns six ProxyAdmin contracts and the EtherfiOFTAdapterUpgradeable. It can upgrade the EtherfiL1SyncPoolETH cross-chain contract and several DummyTokenUpgradeable proxies, and configure LayerZero peers, delegates and rate limits. Unlike the core protocol contracts, these upgrades are not routed through the timelock and take effect immediately.

eth:0x25e821b7197B146F7713C3b89B6A4D8351.name: StakingManager
eth:0x25e821b7197B146F7713C3b89B6A4D8351.description: Protocol-internal contract that owns the UpgradeableBeacon backing all EtherFiNode instances, so it can upgrade the implementation used by every node. It also handles validator key linking and auction settlement. Its own owner is the EtherFi Timelock, so external control traces back to the governance multisig.

eth:0x8B71140AD2e5d1E7018d2a7f8a288BD3CD.name: EtherFiNodesManager
eth:0x8B71140AD2e5d1E7018d2a7f8a288BD3CD.description: Protocol-internal contract that manages validator nodes and their EigenLayer EigenPods: creating pods, queueing and completing ETH withdrawals, running checkpoint proofs, forwarding pod calls, and sweeping funds. It is owned by the EtherFi Timelock.

eth:0x308861A430be4cce5502d0A12724771Fc6.name: LiquidityPool
eth:0x308861A430be4cce5502d0A12724771Fc6.description: Protocol-internal contract that mints and burns eETH shares and registers beacon-chain validators. It holds {{liquidityPoolBalance}} of unstaked ETH and is owned by the EtherFi Timelock.

eth:0xDadEf1fFBFeaAB4f68A9fD181395F68b4e.name: EtherFiRedemptionManager
eth:0xDadEf1fFBFeaAB4f68A9fD181395F68b4e.description: Protocol-internal contract handling eETH redemptions, with {{redemptionManagerCapital}} in direct capital exposure. It can transfer stETH, process withdrawals, and burn eETH shares including for non-ETH withdrawal flows.

eth:0x0EF8fa4760Db8f5Cd4d993f3e3416f30f9.name: EtherFiAdmin
eth:0x0EF8fa4760Db8f5Cd4d993f3e3416f30f9.description: Protocol-internal contract that applies oracle reports: it triggers rebases, pays protocol fees, deposits to recipients, and locks ETH for withdrawals. It has {{etherFiAdminCapital}} in direct capital exposure and is owned by the EtherFi Timelock.

eth:0x62247D29B4B9BECf4BB73E0c722cf6445c.name: RoleRegistry
eth:0x62247D29B4B9BECf4BB73E0c722cf6445c.description: Protocol-internal contract that centralizes role assignments across the protocol and exposes pause, request validation, and ETH/ERC20/ERC721 recovery functions. It is owned by the EtherFi Timelock.

eth:0x3d320286E014C3e1ce99Af6d6B00f0C1D6.name: MembershipManager
eth:0x3d320286E014C3e1ce99Af6d6B00f0C1D6.description: Protocol-internal contract for the membership NFT system that can trigger rebases and process membership NFT withdrawals. It is owned by the EtherFi Timelock.

eth:0x9FFDF407cDe9a93c47611799DA23924Af3.name: Liquifier
eth:0x9FFDF407cDe9a93c47611799DA23924Af3.description: Protocol-internal contract that accepts liquid staking tokens (stETH, cbETH, Curve LP tokens) as deposits and routes them into the protocol. It is owned by the EtherFi Timelock.

eth:0x7d5706f6ef3F89B3951E23e557CDFBC323.name: WithdrawRequestNFT
eth:0x7d5706f6ef3F89B3951E23e557CDFBC323.description: Protocol-internal contract representing pending withdrawal requests as NFTs; it can finalize withdrawals and burn eETH shares. It is owned by the EtherFi Timelock.

eth:0x35e7D6feF6f72aDd3c3e39dEc6d9CCc29e.name: PriorityWithdrawalQueue
eth:0x35e7D6feF6f72aDd3c3e39dEc6d9CCc29e.description: Protocol-internal contract handling priority withdrawals; it can process withdrawals and burn eETH shares. Its permissions trace back to the EtherFi Timelock.

eth:0xD789870beA40D056A4d26055d0bEFcC875.name: EtherfiL1SyncPoolETH
eth:0xD789870beA40D056A4d26055d0bEFcC875.description: Protocol-internal contract that mints eETH against cross-chain deposits bridged via LayerZero and unwraps L2 ETH. Its proxy is upgradeable by the ProxyAdmin Owner Multisig (4-of-7) without timelock delay.

eth:0x00C452aFFee3a17d9Cecc1Bcd2B8d5C763.name: AuctionManager
eth:0x00C452aFFee3a17d9Cecc1Bcd2B8d5C763.description: Protocol-internal contract running the node operator bid auction; it fetches key indices and records auction revenue. It is owned by the EtherFi Timelock.

eth:0x0000000000000000000000000000000000.name: Zero Address (Renounced)
eth:0x0000000000000000000000000000000000.description: Several contract permissions point to the zero address, meaning those ownership or minter roles have been renounced and can no longer be exercised.

eth:0x00000000219ab540356cBB839Cbe05303d.name: Ethereum Beacon Chain Deposit Contract
eth:0x00000000219ab540356cBB839Cbe05303d.description: The canonical Ethereum staking deposit contract. ether.fi calls deposit on it when funding new validators through createBeaconValidators and confirmAndFundBeaconValidators.

eth:0x91E677b07F7AF907ec9a428aafA9fc14a0.name: EigenPodManager (EigenLayer)
eth:0x91E677b07F7AF907ec9a428aafA9fc14a0.description: EigenLayer contract that deploys and manages EigenPods. ether.fi creates one EigenPod per validator node and routes validator creation, checkpoint proofs, consolidations and execution-layer withdrawals through it.

eth:0x39053D51B77DC0d36036Fc1fCc8Cb819df.name: DelegationManager (EigenLayer)
eth:0x39053D51B77DC0d36036Fc1fCc8Cb819df.description: EigenLayer contract managing operator delegations and withdrawals. ether.fi delegates restaked positions, queues and completes withdrawals through it; {{delegationManagerFundsAtRisk}} in capital flows depend on it.

eth:0x858646372CC42E1A627fcE94aa7A7033e7.name: StrategyManager (EigenLayer)
eth:0x858646372CC42E1A627fcE94aa7A7033e7.description: EigenLayer contract that accepts deposits into restaking strategies. The EtherFiRestaker calls depositIntoStrategy on it to restake liquid staking tokens.

eth:0x7750d328b314EfFa365A0402CcfD489B80.name: RewardsCoordinator (EigenLayer)
eth:0x7750d328b314EfFa365A0402CcfD489B80.description: EigenLayer contract distributing restaking rewards. ether.fi sets a rewards claimer on it via setClaimerFor so restaking rewards can be claimed on the protocol's behalf.

eth:0xae7ab96520DE3A18E5e111B5EaAb095312.name: Lido stETH
eth:0xae7ab96520DE3A18E5e111B5EaAb095312.description: Lido's staked ETH token. ether.fi holds and transfers stETH through the EtherFiRestaker and Liquifier, and supports redeeming eETH/weETH against stETH balances.

eth:0x889edC2eDab5f40e902b864aD4d7AdE8E4.name: Lido WithdrawalQueueERC721
eth:0x889edC2eDab5f40e902b864aD4d7AdE8E4.description: Lido's withdrawal queue contract. ether.fi requests and claims stETH withdrawals through it via stEthRequestWithdrawal and stEthClaimWithdrawals.

eth:0xBe9895146f7AF43049ca1c1AE358B0541E.name: Coinbase cbETH
eth:0xBe9895146f7AF43049ca1c1AE358B0541E.description: Coinbase's wrapped staked ETH token, accepted as a deposit asset. Its exchange rate is read by the protocol when valuing cbETH deposits.

eth:0xa2E3356610840701BDf5611a53974510Ae.name: Curve WrapTokenV3ETH
eth:0xa2E3356610840701BDf5611a53974510Ae.description: A Curve-related wrapped ETH token accepted as a deposit asset; the protocol reads its exchange rate and total supply and routes deposits through the EtherFiRestaker.

eth:0x5FAE7E604FC3e24fd43A72867ceBaC94c6.name: Curve Pool
eth:0x5FAE7E604FC3e24fd43A72867ceBaC94c6.description: A Curve pool used by the protocol to quote and execute swaps between deposit assets; the protocol calls get_dy on it during depositWithERC20 flows.

eth:0x1a44076050125825900e736c501f859c50.name: LayerZero EndpointV2
eth:0x1a44076050125825900e736c501f859c50.description: The LayerZero V2 messaging endpoint. ether.fi uses it to send and receive cross-chain messages for the L1 sync pool and OFT token bridging, and to set its messaging delegate.

eth:0xBe010A7e3686FdF65E93344ab664D065A0.name: LayerZero Endpoint Owner
eth:0xBe010A7e3686FdF65E93344ab664D065A0.description: The owner of the LayerZero EndpointV2 and its Send/Receive message libraries. It can register libraries and change the default send and receive libraries used for cross-chain verification, which the protocol's cross-chain messaging depends on.

eth:0xbB2Ea70C9E858123480642Cf96acbcCE13.name: LayerZero SendUln302
eth:0xbB2Ea70C9E858123480642Cf96acbcCE13.description: LayerZero's send-side message library defining how outbound cross-chain messages are encoded and verified for the protocol's cross-chain components.

eth:0xc02Ab410f0734EFa3F14628780e6e69515.name: LayerZero ReceiveUln302
eth:0xc02Ab410f0734EFa3F14628780e6e69515.description: LayerZero's receive-side message library defining the validation of inbound cross-chain messages for the protocol's cross-chain components.

eth:0x308861A430be4cce5502d0A12724771Fc6.name: LiquidityPool (ETH)
eth:0x308861A430be4cce5502d0A12724771Fc6.description: Holds {{liquidityPoolBalance}}, almost entirely unstaked ETH awaiting deployment into validators or redemption. This is the protocol's main liquidity buffer.

eth:0x1B7a4C3797236A1C37f8741c0Be35c2c72.name: EtherFiRestaker (Restaking positions)
eth:0x1B7a4C3797236A1C37f8741c0Be35c2c72.description: Holds {{restakerPositions}} in restaking positions, predominantly ETH staked through Lido with a smaller EigenLayer position. This contract restakes idle protocol liquidity to generate additional yield.

eth:0x35fA164735182de50811E8e2E824cFb9B6.name: eETH Token
eth:0x35fA164735182de50811E8e2E824cFb9B6.description: The protocol's rebasing liquid staking token, with a circulating market capitalization of {{eethMarketCap}}. eETH represents a user's claim on staked ETH plus accrued staking and restaking rewards.

eth:0x6329004E903B7F420245E7aF3f355186f2.name: Treasury (ETH)
eth:0x6329004E903B7F420245E7aF3f355186f2.description: Holds {{treasuryBalance}} in ETH, accumulating protocol fees and revenue. It is owned by the EtherFi Timelock.

codeAndAudits.title: Code & Audits
codeAndAudits.description: Smart contract analysis and audit history
